content: Episodic memory in neural architecture planning agents can be structured as a nested hierarchy of self-similar agent modules, where each stored episodic trace itself acts as a tiny, context-specific curiosity-driven sub-agent that triggers planning only when its activation threshold aligns with the current environment. This nested design eliminates the common bottleneck of irrelevant memory retrieval in large-scale sequential planning by letting each episodic sub-agent actively seek out environmental conditions it evolved to solve.
source_topics: neural architecture memory episodic planning agent
trigger_topic: neural architecture memory episodic planning agent
